- Ask: export template + self-serve filters
- Acceptance criteria: export completes under N minutes; includes required fields; consistent formatting

Keep it tight: translate every bug into a customer-facing “why it matters.”

artifacts.title: Deep work block tomorrow — Integration reliability plan (draft outline)
artifacts.content:

Outline

2) SLIs/SLOs (draft)
- Freshness/latency (p95)

- Duplicate/loop rate (idempotency failures)

3) Alerting gaps

4) Retry/backoff strategy
- Exponential backoff + jitter
- Circuit-breaker behavior on quota exhaustion

5) Idempotency checklist
- Stable event keys
- Upsert semantics
- Dedup window + reconciliation job

6) Next steps
- Identify owners for metrics dashboards
- Propose rollout sequence + instrumentation timeline

Note: keep this doc crisp; I want to be able to shift the block if the morning triage runs long.

artifacts.subject: Credential Pathways ↔ integrations: questions to align on

messages.content:
Hi Zoe — ahead of tomorrow’s Product/Design sync, can you sanity-check these:

1) For pathway-triggered cohort scheduling, do you need a dedicated event type for credential issuance, or should it be represented as metadata on the cohort event?
2) Edge cases: re-issuance, revoked credentials, and timezone changes after scheduling.
3) Any constraints from the external calendar connectors we should design around?

If you have a preferred sequence for rollout (API first vs UI), let us know so we come prepared.

Thanks — yes, these are the right questions.

Initial thoughts for tomorrow:
- I’d prefer a first-class issuance event if it needs independent lifecycle + auditing; otherwise metadata is fine, but we should confirm what downstream systems expect.
- Let’s explicitly walk through re-issue + revoke scenarios and how they propagate.
- Connector constraints: idempotency + retry behavior matters a lot; we should avoid designs that create ambiguous “same event, new meaning” updates.

I’ll bring a checklist of integration edge cases and we can agree on API contracts + rollout order in the session.

artifacts.title: Debug plan: webhook retry spikes (Teams)
artifacts.content:
Goal for tomorrow’s deep work block: narrow retry spikes to one of: signature verification failures, transient 429 handling, downstream timeouts, or duplicate deliveries.

Checklist
- Add structured logs:
  - signature verification outcome + reason codes
  - tenant/org identifiers (non-PII), request id/correlation id
  - response status + retry decision path
- Verify 429/backoff logic:
  - ensure we honor Retry-After where present
  - cap max attempts; confirm jitter
- Pull sample traces around elevated retry windows.
- Compare “delivered → processed” latency distributions.

Output expected by end of block
- one-page summary in Jira comment + next steps
- if needed, open follow-up ticket for deeper instrumentation

See you at the review tomorrow.

artifacts.subject: UI language for confidence / uncertainty (dispatcher-friendly)

messages.content:
Hi Olivia,

Ahead of our product alignment, could you send your latest thinking on how we should present “confidence” without implying the model is guessing? Priya and I are debating whether to show a simple label vs a tooltip with more detail.

If you have draft thresholds in mind, that would help too.

Aisha — sharing a first pass below (keeping this within the working group for now).

Proposal:
- Primary UI: 3-tier label (e.g., High / Medium / Low) with neutral wording (“Signal strength”) rather than “confidence.”
- Secondary detail: tooltip that explains what tends to drive lower signal (sparse recent vessel position updates, unusual queue patterns, etc.)

Thresholds (placeholder until we sanity-check with real dispatcher workflows):
- High: stable inputs + low variance in recent predictions
- Medium: one risk factor present
- Low: multiple risk factors or recent distribution shift

I’d like tomorrow’s session to end with agreed copy + an explicit rule for when we *don’t* show a number. Let’s avoid over-explaining in the main flow—dispatchers need an at-a-glance cue.

Also: please don’t forward the calendar invite text around; I’m trying to keep some of my schedule details tight.

artifacts.title: Customer feedback readout — synthesis bullets + next experiments
artifacts.content:
Bring to readout tomorrow:

What users misunderstand
- Delay attribution: people assume “delay reason” is definitive; they miss confidence/unknown states
- Berth changes: operators want to see *who/what* triggered the change and when

Metrics they trust
- Arrivals/departures when sourced consistently; skepticism spikes when fields disagree across feeds
- “Last updated” timestamp matters more than we assume (operators use it to judge whether to act)

Workflow friction
- Too many terms that mean different things per port (berth/terminal naming)
- Exceptions list is actionable only if it’s obvious what can be fixed vs. what is informational

Proposed UX experiments
- Add lightweight confidence/quality indicator for key fields (timestamp + identifiers)
- Clarify exception categories: “needs your action” vs. “monitor”
- Delay attribution explainer: short inline rationale + link to details (avoid wall of text)

Decision needed in the meeting
- Pick 1–2 experiments to validate next (tie into sprint tickets / handoff later)

artifacts.subject: Re: Data ingestion + licensing mapping — quick alignment before tomorrow

messages.content: Fatima — before tomorrow’s check-in, do you have a view on the retention window we should assume for partner feeds, and how strict the licensing constraints are for customer entitlements? Want to make sure engineering doesn’t overbuild the wrong access controls.

Yes—let’s treat tomorrow’s session as a working alignment and lock a baseline we can implement quickly.

My current take (happy to refine live):
- Retention: start conservative with a clearly documented default window; we can expand once we have partner sign-off in writing.
- Entitlements: assume we’ll need feature-level gating (not just org-level) so we can keep derived outputs available even when raw fields can’t be exposed.
- Access controls: build for “allowed uses” tags per dataset so we can map licensing terms to API scopes without bespoke logic.

Also, please keep partner-specific terms out of tickets that have broad visibility—we can capture details in the smaller meeting notes.

See you tomorrow.

artifacts.title: UI alignment tomorrow — how we explain AIS confidence + provenance
artifacts.content:
For the 16:00 Customer Applications alignment, aim to leave with agreed copy/tooltips.

Decisions needed:
- Terminology: “confidence”, “quality”, or “data completeness” (avoid over-promising)
- Provenance: where we attribute port events (source label + “derived from…” language)
- When to show uncertainty: low-coverage regions, sparse timestamps, conflicting port calls
- UI behavior: tooltip vs. expandable details; whether to expose raw timestamps

Draft tooltip copy options:
A) “Estimated from vessel position signals and port event detection. Accuracy varies by region and signal frequency.”
B) “Derived from vessel tracking and port activity data. Some events may be approximate when signals are intermittent.”

Prep: bring 2 screenshots showing how it looks in the app today + one example of a confusing port call.

Note: keep wording consistent with what we can actually support under partner terms.

artifacts.title: UI alignment (customer app): how we explain AIS-derived confidence
artifacts.content:
For the Customer Applications alignment tomorrow:

Goal: agree on how we present confidence + provenance without overpromising.

Open decisions
- Confidence indicator: discrete buckets vs. continuous percentage
- Tooltip copy: avoid implying certainty; reference “signal quality” / “recent observations” language
- Provenance: show “source type” and last update time, but keep partner naming out of customer-facing strings
- Port event lineage: what we can explain plainly (e.g., “inferred from vessel movement + port event data”) vs. what stays internal

Draft copy options
- Badge: “Estimated” / “High confidence” / “Limited signal”
- Tooltip: “This estimate is derived from vessel position signals and port activity indicators. Coverage can vary by region and time.”

Bring to meeting
- 2 screenshots with proposed placements (no partner identifiers)
- Quick decision list to close in 30 mins if needed (timing can flex a little if earlier items run over).
